facts.text: Autism is also known as autism spectrum disorder (ASD) and is classified as a neurodevelopmental disorder.
The World Health Organization estimates that about 1 in 100 children were diagnosed with autism between 2012 and 2021.
Autism diagnoses have risen since the 1990s, largely because of broader diagnostic criteria, greater awareness, and wider access to assessment.
Autism is a spectrum disorder, meaning presentations vary and support needs range from minimal to the person being non-speaking or needing 24-hour care.
Features of autism are present from early childhood and the condition typically persists throughout life.
The exact causes of autism are unknown, with genetics likely being the largest contributing factor.
There is no cure for autism; from the perspective of neurodiversity, curing or otherwise treating autism may not be an appropriate goal.
About 85% of autistic people need support with independent living in adulthood.
Among children diagnosed with autism before age 6, about 9 in 10 continue to meet diagnostic criteria one year later.
Mortality among autistic people is approximately twice that of the general population, particularly among those with co-occurring neurologic or psychiatric conditions.
Research indicates that autistic people are significantly more likely to be LGBTQ than the general population.
Autistic adults are more likely to be unemployed and have greater mental health service needs than the general population.
Factors associated with a higher likelihood of independent living in adulthood include developing spoken language before age six, having an IQ above 50, and possessing marketable skills.
Repetitive behaviors in autism, also called stimming, are restricted or repetitive patterns of interests, activities, or behaviors.
Environmental activist Greta Thunberg has spoken favorably about her autism diagnosis, saying autism can be a source of life purpose as well as the basis of careers, hobbies, and friendships.
Bill Gates, entrepreneur and co-founder of Microsoft, has written that if he were growing up today, he probably would be diagnosed on the autism spectrum.
Most professionals believe that race, ethnicity, and socioeconomic background have limited effect on the occurrence of autism.
Autism-related events include World Autism Awareness Day, Autism Sunday, Autistic Pride Day, and Autreat.
